'''Paul McNicholas''' (born 26 May 1975) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] (two spells), [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]], and [[Hull F.C.]], as a {{rlp|pr}}, or {{rlp|sr}}.

==Background==
McNicholas was born in [[Sydney]], Australia.

==Playing career==
McNicholas made his first grade debut for South Sydney in round 1 of the [[1996 ARL season]] against [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] at [[Brookvale Oval]]. He played off the bench in Souths 44-6 loss. In the [[1999 NRL season]], McNicholas played 15 games for South Sydney including what was meant to be their final ever game when they played against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. Souths lost the match 34-16 and were ejected from the competition ahead of the [[2000 NRL season]] due to not meeting the NRL's controversial criteria. For the 2000 season, McNicholas signed a contract to join Cronulla. In 2001, he played 20 games including Cronulla's preliminary final loss to [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]]. When South Sydney were re-admitted back to the competition in 2002, McNicholas signed for the club and played in their first game since the readmission which was a 40-6 loss against arch-rivals the [[Sydney Roosters]]. In 2003, McNicholas joined [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] and featured in eight games. He was the second tallest player In his time with the [[National Rugby League]] just behind behemoth Prop [[Matt Parsons]]. McNicholas spent the final two years of his career playing for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2018/06/12/heart-on-their-sleeve/|title=Heart on their Sleeve|website=rabbitohs.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/brothers-in-arms-how-bulldogs-helped-hunted-bunnies-bounce-back-from-the-brink-of-extinction-20120921-26c5z.html/|title=Brothers in arms: how Bulldogs helped hunted Bunnies bounce back from the brink of extinction|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=21 September 2012 }}</ref>

==International honors==
McNicholas won three caps for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in 2005–2006 while at Hull.<ref>{{cite book| first1= Graham| last1= Williams| first2= Peter| last2= Lush| first3= David| last3= Farrar| title= The British Rugby League Records Book| publisher= London League| isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6| year= 2009| pages= 104–105}}</ref>
